https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōtomo no Fukei-qq]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}

== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}

revision.text:
https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0&action=edit
[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Fukei}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ōtomo no Fukei-->[[Category:Ōtomo no Fukei]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E5%90%B9%E8%B2%A0]] '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: - '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. - '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. - '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|天武天皇12年8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' - {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) - {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}} was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=draft}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=draft}}. Fukei's posthumous rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=draft}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|12=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=draft}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=draft}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=draft}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=draft}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=draft}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=draft}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=draft}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=draft}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=draft}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=draft}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴 吹負 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[天武天皇]]12年[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]（[[683年]][[9月1日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 男吹負、小吹負 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[贈位|贈]][[大錦中]]・[[常陸国司|常道頭]] | 主君 = [[天武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴]][[連]] | 父母 = 父：[[大伴咋]] | 兄弟 = [[大伴長徳|長徳]]、[[大伴馬来田|馬来田]]、'''吹負'''、智仙娘、真広 | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴牛養|牛養]]、[[大伴祖父麻呂|祖父麻呂]] | 特記事項 = }}
'''大伴 吹負'''（おおとも の ふけい<ref>旧仮名遣いでは「おほとも の ふけひ」</ref>）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の人物。名は'''男吹負'''、'''小吹負'''（おふけい<ref>旧仮名遣いでは「をふけひ」</ref>）とも記される。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。[[大徳 (冠位)|大徳]]・[[大伴咋]]の子。[[官位]]は[[贈位|贈]][[大錦中]]・[[常陸国司|常道頭]]。
[[672年]]の[[壬申の乱]]で、吹負は大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）側に立って兵を挙げ、[[飛鳥]]の倭京に集結中の敵軍を乗っ取って、倭（[[大和国|大和]]）の方面の将軍になった。及楽山で敗れたが、葦池と中つ道で連勝し、最終的に大和を制圧して[[難波]]に進出した。
== 経歴 == [[大伴氏]]は飛鳥時代の有力氏族だが、[[孝徳天皇|孝徳朝]]の大臣・[[大伴長徳]]が没してからは不遇であった。[[天智天皇|天智朝]]では兄・[[大伴馬来田|馬来田]]と吹負は病を称して自宅に退いた。二人は次の天皇は大海人皇子に違いないと考え、天智天皇の死後、挙兵しようとして、1-2名の同族と諸々の豪傑、あわせて数十人を集めた<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年6月26日条</ref>。
天武天皇元年（[[672年]]）6月24日に大海人皇子が挙兵のために東に向かうと、馬来田はその後を追い、吹負は家に留まった。大友皇子（[[弘文天皇]]）は、大海人皇子の反乱を鎮圧するため、使者三人を倭京（飛鳥の古い都）に遣わし、倭京の留守司[[高坂王]]と共に兵を集めさせた。吹負は別の留守司[[坂上熊毛]]と謀議して、吹負が[[高市皇子]]と偽って敵の本営に近づき、同時に熊毛と一部の倭直（[[倭漢氏]]）が内応するという計画を立てた。6月29日にまず[[秦熊]]を使者として走らせ、高市皇子が来たと叫ばせて、兵士に動揺が走ったところに吹負ら数十騎が馳せつけた。これに熊毛らが呼応すると、他の軍士はこれに従った。大津から派遣された使者で、武器を大友皇子のために輸送する任についていた[[穂積百足]]は殺された。他の二人の使者である[[穂積五百枝]]と[[物部日向]]は捕らえられたが、後に赦されて吹負の軍に加わった。高坂王・[[稚狭王]]も味方になった。こうして倭京の軍の指揮権を奪取した吹負は成功を大海人皇子に報じ、将軍に任命された。周辺地域の豪傑が吹負の下に集まり、近江を襲う作戦を立てた<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年6月29日条</ref>。
吹負の軍は7月1日に及楽（[[奈良市|奈良]]）に向かった。途中、河内から敵の大軍が来るとの情報を得た吹負は、[[坂本財]]らに兵力各数百の三部隊を委ねて西方に分派した<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年7月1日条</ref>。3日に及楽山に陣した吹負は、[[荒田尾赤麻呂]]の言をいれて、古京に守備の兵力を割いた<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年7月3日条</ref>。4日に及楽山で[[大野果安]]が率いる近江軍と会戦し、敗れて逃げた。その頃、西に派遣された部隊も敗れて退却した。果安は南に進んで倭京を遠望したが、伏兵があるのではないかと考えて引き上げた<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年7月4日条</ref>。
逃げた吹負は[[置始菟]]の騎兵隊に墨坂で出会った。これは美濃国から吹負のために送られた[[紀阿閉麻呂]]ら数万の軍の先遣隊であった。金綱井で敗兵を集めた吹負は、まず西に出撃して[[壱伎韓国]]の軍と葦池のほとりで戦い、[[来目]]を先頭にした騎兵の突撃で勝利を得た。ついで北に備えて[[犬養五十君]]の軍と戦った。この戦いで吹負が直率した中つ道の軍は、[[廬井鯨]]の小部隊の突撃を受けて苦戦した。吹負の本営を[[徳麻呂]]らが支える間に、右翼の[[三輪高市麻呂]]と置始菟の部隊が回り込み、鯨を撃退することができた。吹負は倭京に引きあげ、これ以後近江軍は[[奈良盆地]]に進攻してこなくなった。
以上の一連の戦いの終わり頃に、[[近江国]]では味方の[[村国男依]]らが前進を始めた。彼らは7日から交戦を始め、連戦連勝して大津に迫った。7月22日に吹負は他の諸将を北進させ、自らは難波に向かった。難波の小郡で、以西の諸国の国司に命じて官の鍵・[[駅鈴]]・伝印を送らせた<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇元年7月22日条</ref>。同日、近江では大友皇子の軍が瀬田で敗れ、翌日に皇子は自殺した。
壬申の乱の戦域は大別して近江と倭（大和）の二つあり、吹負はその一方の主将として活躍して大きな功績を立てた。しかしながら吹負の乱後の活動は『[[日本書紀]]』に記されない。相当の賞があったはずだが、それも見えない。贈位の大錦中は、低くはないが他の壬申の功臣と比べれば決して高くもない。後述の『[[続日本紀]]』の記事から、天武天皇の代に吹負が常道頭（[[常陸国]]の[[守]]）を務めたことが知られる。
天武天皇12年（[[683年]]）8月5日卒去。壬申の乱における功労によって、[[大錦中]]の冠位を贈られた。
『続日本紀』は[[天平]]勝宝元年（[[749年]]）閏5月29日の[[大伴牛養]]死亡の記事に、「贈大錦中小吹負の男」と記す。また[[宝亀]]8年（[[777年]]）8月19日の[[大伴古慈斐]]の死亡記事は「飛鳥朝の常道頭、贈大錦中小吹負の孫」と書き、あわせて古慈斐が[[大伴祖父麻呂|祖父麻呂]]の子であることも記す。
== 系譜 == *父：[[大伴咋]] *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[大伴牛養]]（?-749） **男子：[[大伴祖父麻呂]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の ふけい}} [[Category:大伴氏|ふけい]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:683年没]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|qid=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|qid=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|qid=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|qq=|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|qq=|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|qq=|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|qq=|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|qq=|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|qq=|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|qq=|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|qq=|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|qq=|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|qq=|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|qq=|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|qq=|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|qq=|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|qq=|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|qq=|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|qq=|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.

However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]

Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]

<!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--><!--Ōtomo no Fukei--> [[Category:Jinshin War]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]]
