<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]

For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] was [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]]. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]. During the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432333]] Wikidata name: Fukei Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}

He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}
{{translated page|ja|大伴吹負|version=96722509|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358}}}}

His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Yamato Capital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358|1=Ōtomo no Kui}}}}
'''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Q11387076|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
== Genealogy == * '''Father:''' {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}} * '''Children:''' ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} (?-749) ** {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}
== References == {{reflist|2}}
== See also == * {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}
{{translated page|ja|大伴吹負|version=96722509|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴吹負]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0683|9|1}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikai|WD=Q11432344|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|WD=Q11432349|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Kui|WD=Q30932358|1=Ōtomo no Kui}}}}
'''Ōtomo no Fukei''' (大伴 吹負) was a military leader during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} in Japan. His name is also recorded as '''Otoko Fukei''' or '''Ko Fukei''' (小吹負). His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}. He was a son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Kujii|ja|大伴咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kujii|1=Ōtomo no Kui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30932358}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Ōdoku|ja|大徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdoku|1=Daedeok|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935428}}. Fukei's posthumous Rank was {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}, and he also served as the governor of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} of 672, Fukei supported Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), leading forces to secure Yamato Province and eventually pushing into Naniwa.
== Background == The {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} was a prominent lineage during the Asuka period. However, following the death of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}, their influence waned. During Emperor Tenji's reign, Fukei and his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Makarita|ja|大伴馬来田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Makarita|1=Ōtomo no Makuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432359}} claimed illness to retire from public life. Privately, they anticipated that Prince Ōama would ascend the throne. After Emperor Tenji’s death, Fukei and Makarita gathered a small force of loyalists to support Ōama.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 26th day.</ref>
== Military Campaigns == In the Jinshin War, Fukei played a crucial role in the Yamato front. On June 24, 672, Prince Ōama declared rebellion, and Fukei remained in Yamato while his brother Makarita followed Ōama eastward. The opposing Emperor Kobun dispatched emissaries to organize defenses at the {{ill|Yamato Capital|ja|倭京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Capital|1=Q11387076|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387076}} under the stewardship of {{ill|Takasaka no Ō|ja|高坂王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka no Ō|1=Prince Takasaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669026}}. Fukei collaborated with {{ill|Sakanoue no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Kumake|1=Sakagami no Kumake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424804}} to orchestrate a defection within the Yamato garrison. Fukei impersonated Prince Takeda, spreading false reports of imminent reinforcements. On June 29, Fukei and Kumake’s coordinated efforts led to the collapse of Kobun’s forces at Yamato. Key enemy commanders, including {{ill|Hozumi no Momomochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momomochi|1=Hozumi no Momotari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597083}}, were killed or captured, while others joined Fukei’s Ranks.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Tenmu 1st year, 6th month, 29th day.</ref>
Fukei then led forces to confront the Kobun army in several engagements: * '''Ōtagi Mountain (July 3–4):''' Fukei suffered a defeat against {{ill|Ōno no Kaeyasu|ja|大野果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Kaeyasu|1=Ōno no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439656}} but regrouped at Mutsusaka with reinforcements from Mino Province. * '''Rebounding at Ashiike and Nakatsu-michi:''' Fukei’s forces decisively defeated enemy detachments, consolidating control over Yamato Province. * '''Advance to Naniwa (July 22):''' Fukei secured Naniwa and coordinated with regional officials to ensure allegiance to Ōama.
== Death and Legacy == Ōtomo no Fukei passed away on {{nihongo|August 5, 683|Emperor Tenmu12nd year 8月5日|}}. For his contributions during the Jinshin War, he was posthumously granted the Rank of {{ill|Jū Daikin Middle|ja|大錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jū Daikin Middle|1=Daikin-chū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439800}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Tenpyō Shōhō 1, 5th intercalary month, 29th day.</ref> His sons, {{ill|Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|ja|大伴牛養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Ushikatsu|1=Ōtomo no Ushikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432344}} and {{ill|Ōtomo no Sobei|ja|大伴祖父麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sobei|1=Ōtomo no Ōjimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432349}}, continued his lineage, with descendants maintaining prominence in the Nara and Heian periods.
